name: Flipkart-Style Product Search Engine — Parallel Data Fetcher
scenario: When Flipkart displays a search results page, it fans out to multiple services simultaneously: product catalog, ratings service, inventory, pricing engine, recommendation engine. These run in parallel and the page assembles when all respond. Build this fan-out pattern using ExecutorService and Future<T>.
description: A parallel search engine that fans out to 5 simulated data services concurrently using a thread pool. Demonstrates invokeAll, Future result collection, timeout handling, graceful degradation on service failures, and proper executor shutdown.

core.description: `SearchResult` record: productId, name, price, rating, inStock, description. At minimum 4 fields required.

`DataService` interface with `Callable<SearchResult> fetchFor(String productId, String query)`. At least 3 implementations: CatalogService (simulated 100-200ms), RatingsService (50-150ms), InventoryService (80-120ms).
`SearchEngine` with a `FixedThreadPool` of size 5. `search(String query, List<String> productIds)` method: submit one Callable per product-service combination, collect via invokeAll.
Timeout handling: each service call has max 500ms timeout via future.get(500, MILLISECONDS). Timed-out services are treated as unavailable (use default/fallback value).
Graceful degradation: if a service throws ExecutionException, the result is partial (null fields) but search still returns results for other products/services.
print results sorted by rating descending. Print per-search timing (parallel vs estimated sequential).

bonus.description: Add ScheduledExecutorService that runs a cache-warmup task every 30 seconds (simulated with shorter interval for demo). Print 'Cache warmed at HH:mm:ss'.
Add invokeAny-based 'fast path': if ANY service returns a result in under 100ms, immediately render a partial result to show the user something fast, then fill in the rest.

Add Circuit Breaker pattern: if a service fails 3 times consecutively (AtomicInteger failure counter), mark it as 'open' (volatile boolean circuitOpen) and skip it for 5 seconds, then half-open to retry.

data_to_use:
Products to search: P001(Mouse), P002(Keyboard), P003(Monitor), P004(USB Hub), P005(Headphones)
Query: 'computer accessories'

Service delays:
- CatalogService: 100-200ms
- RatingsService: 50-150ms  
- InventoryService: 80-120ms
example_output:
=== Flipkart Search: 'computer accessories' ===

Fetching from 3 services for 5 products (15 parallel calls)...
Completed in 203ms (sequential estimate: 2250ms, speedup: 11.1x)

=== Results (sorted by rating) ===

Keyboard     ₹1999   ★4.7  In Stock  - Mechanical keys
Headphones   ₹2999   ★4.5  In Stock  - Noise cancelling
Mouse        ₹799    ★4.2  In Stock  - Wireless
USB Hub      ₹499    ★4.0  In Stock  - 7-port

=== Service Performance ===
CatalogService:   5/5 successful
RatingsService:   5/5 successful
InventoryService: 5/5 successful

criteria: get(500, MILLISECONDS) on each future (10 pts)
TimeoutException caught and degraded gracefully (10 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Key design: 15 parallel calls (5 products × 3 services). Use invokeAll for all 15. Each future's result merges into the SearchResult for that product. Speedup = (sum of all service times) / actual elapsed. Partial results: if a service times out, use default value for that field.
submission_instructions: Save as FlipkartSearch.java. Push to GitHub. This demonstrates production-grade parallel service fan-out.
tips_for_success: Create 15 Callable tasks (5 products × 3 services), submit all via invokeAll
Map each future back to (productId, serviceType) to merge results
Merge strategy: CatalogService provides name/price/description; RatingsService provides rating; InventoryService provides inStock
Timeout: future.get(500, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS) — wrap in try-catch(TimeoutException)
Speedup calculation: sum(all service average times) / actual elapsed
